title: practical english usage - Part 259
content:
Indian pronunciation of English is generally syllable-timed rather than example needful, thrice (three times), miscreant (criminal).

Others are current in India although they are no longer used elsewhere, for issueless (having no children), prepone (the opposite of postpone), teacheress.

English words are special to Indian English, for example headbath (shampoo), English outside India, for example bungalow, cheetah, yoga, nirvana.
